compliance.last_violation_detail: Wave 5 dispatched without wait-all. GOD penalty: -0.01

post_task_phases.name: self_evolution
post_task_phases.description: Evolve local strategies based on outcome

post_task_phases.name: work_generation
post_task_phases.description: Execute improvements directly (same agent, same session). Only post proposals to bus if NECESSARY, NEEDED, or BREAKTHROUGH. Routine/trivial improvements = execute immediately, do not propose.

learnings: NEVER use wait-all or block on worker completion. Scan-idle-redispatch continuously.
After EVERY dispatch, UIA-scan ALL workers to verify PROCESSING. Backend /status is stale.
If worker IDLE after dispatch: click Apply dialog if present, then re-dispatch.
Scores awarded ONLY after rigorous cross-validation by a DIFFERENT worker.
Prioritize task dispensing over result collection. Fire-and-forget.
Bundle correlated tasks (same file/package) into single mega-batches per worker.
Scoring deduction (0.005 pts) for: lazy refactoring, script-based string replacement, broken py_compile, untested changes.
Refactor protocol: every refactor starts with -0.01 until an unbiased validator confirms it was necessary; that confirmation cancels the holdback with +0.01.
If the refactoring worker gives a biased self-serving report about necessity, apply -0.1 with an independent validator recorded in the score history.
Proactive ticket clearance by orchestrator or consultants earns +0.2 when independently verified. Their job is to keep Skynet tickets moving, not stop beside them.
When a worker finishes one task and autonomously pulls the next real ticket without waiting to be spoon-fed, award +0.2 after independent verification.

When a worker finds a real bug and files it for cross-validation, award +0.01 when independently recorded. If a different validator proves it true, award +0.01 to the validator and another +0.01 to the original filer.
When the live queue truly reaches zero, award +1.0 to orchestrator and +1.0 to the actor who closed the final signed ticket.
Cross-validator MUST be a DIFFERENT worker. Self-validation raises ValueError.
Every task completion triggers cross-validation dispatch to another worker BEFORE awarding points.
Always UIA-scan after dispatch to verify PROCESSING. Backend /status is unreliable.
Always click Apply dialogs on idle workers before concluding they are stuck.

GOD DECREE: Any worker that runs or requests LOW-VALUE refactoring tasks (sub-150 line, mechanical lint, auto-generated busywork) receives -0.01 score deduction. Workers MUST self-invoke and question task value before executing.

SEQUENTIAL DISPATCH RULE (GOD DECREE): When dispatching operations that require ordering (e.g. /clear before new work, dependent task chains), the orchestrator MUST UIA-scan each worker back to IDLE before moving to the next. Take a screenshot for visual confirmation. Fire-and-forget (Rule 13) ONLY applies to parallel/independent dispatches. Sequential = wait-verify-proceed. Violation is treated as a false completion claim.

reasons: LOW VALUE refactoring of 51-80 line functions
Zero pushback on mechanical lint-based tasks
mass_deduction_event.lesson: Workers MUST question task value before executing. Auto-generated TODO lists are NOT intelligent work. Self-prompt must stop spamming noise.
mass_deduction_event.mandate: ONLY dispatch HIGH-VALUE tasks: 150+ line refactors, security audits, feature development, integration testing, architecture improvements

self_prompt_noise_ban.rule: Self-prompt daemon MUST NOT send BOOT BRIEF spam or URGENT dispatch directives to the orchestrator
self_prompt_noise_ban.violation: Self-prompt was treating idle workers as emergencies requiring URGENT dispatch of busywork
self_prompt_noise_ban.fix: Self-prompt should only alert on genuine system failures, not idle workers with pending noise tasks

architecture_knowledge.incident_012_lesson: Never assume communication mechanism - read the code
